    Limitation: Because the work mainly provides controlled empirical evaluation rather than a new architecture or training objective, it leaves open which specific modeling or data interventions would most improve semantic correctness and robustness.

    Niche: This paper targets probabilistic modeling and next-element prediction for source code in the presence of large vocabularies and structured program context.
    Limitation: Because prediction quality depends on a hand-constructed decision-tree factorization over mostly local contextual cues, the model may miss longer-range program dependencies and is constrained by the chosen tree structure.

    Limitation: Because the model is encoder-only and trained mainly on token sequences with limited explicit structural supervision, it is less suited to generative tasks and may underuse deeper program structure beyond surface token patterns.

    Limitation: Because the system depends on phrase-based alignments over a structure-preserving but SMT-compatible code representation rather than deeper semantic analysis, it is limited in capturing long-range program semantics and more complex code patterns.
